WaitingTimeVsEnergy_03_07_2026_12_01_54 — Interactive Experiment Report

Objectives: Average Waiting Time (s) vs Energy (kWh) (both minimized) · Plots rendered with matplotlib (EAF publication style, as plot_scenario_pareto.py) · generated 2026-07-07 12:36 UTC

Display

PowerCap Views

Mode
Tier
Algorithm

Only available for PowerCeiling experiments — this data has no _PC<tier> arms.

Algorithms — colour & visibility

GA Dominance (Waiting Time)
GA Dominance (Energy)
SA Dominance (Waiting Time)
SA Dominance (Energy)
NSGA-II
SPEA-II
AMOSA
Universal Pareto

Colour and visibility apply to every plot, legend entry and table at once. Each plot has its own title box and an axis-swap button.

Pareto Fronts

Multi-objective algorithms: 50% empirical attainment surface with [25%, 75%] band across seeds. Single-objective algorithms: median with IQR error bars. Axes globally normalized to ideal (0,0) and nadir (1,1); raw units on the secondary axes. HV in the legend is the pooled median [IQR] per algorithm across ALL (scenario, seed) runs, computed from the HV column of scenario_<N>_performance_metrics.csv — the same figure plot_scenario_pareto.py publishes. It intentionally differs from the per-scenario HV means shown in the Quality Indicators section below. The Points-view button on each plot switches to a plain raw-unit scatter of every algorithm’s pooled Pareto set, with the Universal Pareto set overlaid as open black rings — the coloured marker inside a ring is the algorithm that contributed that point.

X: Average Waiting Time (s) / Y: Energy (kWh)
X: Average Waiting Time (s) / Y: Energy (kWh)
X: Average Waiting Time (s) / Y: Energy (kWh)

Quality Indicators — Plots

Bars are per-algorithm MEAN values per scenario, taken verbatim from scenario_<N>_performance_metrics.csv (the Java-computed indicators — NOT the pooled HV_fixed used in the Pareto plot legends), plus the Universal_Pareto reference. GD and IGD of the universal set are 0 by definition (it is the reference front). For Pareto contribution, the CSV MEAN row holds the TOTAL number of universal-front points contributed across all seeds (the universal bar is the front size). Swapping axes turns the chart horizontal.

X: scenario / Y: HV (mean)
X: scenario / Y: GD (mean)
X: scenario / Y: IGD (mean)
X: scenario / Y: ParetoContribution (total)

Quality Indicators — Tables

Values verbatim from scenario_<N>_performance_metrics.csv (the same tables the scripts in scripts/ consume): one row per seed plus MEAN and STDDEV, and the Universal_Pareto reference row. Click a seeds button to expand.
